tests.condition: Rule-out test in low / intermediate pre-test probability

citation.reference: Patel P, et al. Systematic review and meta-analysis of test accuracy for the diagnosis of suspected pulmonary embolism. Blood Adv. 2020;4(18):4296-4311 (61 studies).
citation.sourceQuote: The pooled estimates for D-dimer sensitivity and specificity were 0.97 (95% confidence interval [CI], 0.96-0.98) and 0.41 (95% CI, 0.36-0.46) respectively.
citation.context: Quantitative D-dimer; patients with suspected first/recurrent PE. Values vary by assay (ELISA vs latex vs whole-blood) and increase in specificity with age-adjusted thresholds.
citation.notes: High sensitivity / low specificity makes a NEGATIVE result a strong rule-out (low LR-). DOI/PMID must be re-verified by faculty before teaching use.

tests.condition: Confirmatory imaging, typically after a positive D-dimer or high pre-test probability

citation.reference: Patel P, et al. Systematic review and meta-analysis of test accuracy for the diagnosis of suspected pulmonary embolism. Blood Adv. 2020;4(18):4296-4311 (61 studies).
citation.sourceQuote: CTPA sensitivity and specificity were 0.94 (95% CI, 0.89-0.97) and 0.98 (95% CI, 0.97-0.99), respectively.
citation.context: CT pulmonary angiography in suspected PE; performance depends on scanner generation and image quality.
citation.notes: High specificity makes a POSITIVE result strongly rule-in (high LR+).

tests.condition: Indirect test for PE via detection of proximal DVT

citation.reference: Patel P, et al. Systematic review and meta-analysis of test accuracy for the diagnosis of suspected pulmonary embolism. Blood Adv. 2020;4(18):4296-4311 (61 studies).
citation.sourceQuote: CUS sensitivity and specificity were 0.49 (95% CI, 0.31-0.66) and 0.96 (95% CI, 0.95-0.98), respectively.
citation.context: Compression ultrasonography detects proximal DVT as a surrogate for PE; low sensitivity means a negative study does NOT exclude PE.
citation.notes: Illustrates a rule-in (not rule-out) test: high specificity, low sensitivity.

citation.reference: Bhatt M, et al. Diagnosis of deep vein thrombosis of the lower extremity: a systematic review and meta-analysis of test accuracy. Blood Adv. 2020;4(7):1250-1264 (43 studies).
citation.sourceQuote: For any suspected DVT, the pooled estimates for sensitivity and specificity of proximal compression US were 90.1% (95% confidence interval [CI], 86.5-92.8) and 98.5% (95% CI, 97.6-99.1), respectively.
citation.context: Proximal (not whole-leg) compression ultrasonography for lower-extremity DVT; whole-leg and serial US have different characteristics.
citation.notes: High specificity makes a POSITIVE result a strong rule-in. Verify against source before teaching use.

tests.condition: Rule-out in low / intermediate pre-test probability

citation.reference: Bhatt M, et al. Diagnosis of deep vein thrombosis of the lower extremity: a systematic review and meta-analysis of test accuracy. Blood Adv. 2020;4(7):1250-1264 (43 studies).
citation.sourceQuote: For D-dimer, pooled estimates were 96.1% (95% CI, 92.6-98.0) and 35.7% (95% CI, 29.5-42.4).
citation.context: Quantitative D-dimer for suspected lower-extremity DVT; low specificity means a positive result is non-specific.
citation.notes: High sensitivity / low specificity → strong rule-out when negative.

citation.reference: Booth RA, et al. Performance of BNP and NT-proBNP for diagnosis of heart failure in primary care patients: a systematic review. Heart Fail Rev. 2014;19(4):439-451.
citation.sourceQuote: Pooled sensitivity and specificity of BNP and NT-proBNP using the lowest [0.85 (sensitivity) and 0.54 (specificity)], optimal (0.80 and 0.61), and manufacturers' (0.74 and 0.67) cutpoints showed good performance for diagnosing HF.
citation.context: Primary-care populations; performance is cutpoint-dependent (values shown are the 'optimal' cutpoint). Specificity is lower and more variable than sensitivity.
citation.notes: Best used as a rule-out (high sensitivity at low thresholds). Acute-care thresholds differ; verify before teaching use.

citation.reference: Booth RA, et al. Performance of BNP and NT-proBNP for diagnosis of heart failure in primary care patients: a systematic review. Heart Fail Rev. 2014;19(4):439-451.
citation.sourceQuote: Similar performance was seen for NT-proBNP: lowest (0.90 and 0.50), optimal (0.86 and 0.58), and manufacturers' (0.82 and 0.58) cutpoints.
citation.context: Primary-care populations; 'optimal' cutpoint shown. No statistically significant difference in accuracy vs BNP.
citation.notes: Rule-out test; threshold- and population-dependent. Verify before teaching use.

tests.condition: ED patients with suspected ACS without ST-elevation; serial 0h/1h algorithm

citation.reference: Burgos LM, et al. Performance of the ESC 0/1-hour algorithm in the diagnosis of myocardial infarction with high-sensitivity cardiac troponin: systematic review and meta-analysis. Eur Heart J Acute Cardiovasc Care. 2020;10(3):279-286 (11 studies, 19,213 patients).
citation.sourceQuote: Summary sensitivity and specificity in diagnosing AMI were 99% (95% CI 98-99%;63%) and 91% (95% CI 91-92%;96%) respectively.
citation.context: Emergency-department suspected ACS, AMI prevalence ~11%; the 0h/1h SERIAL hs-cTn algorithm, not a single draw. The 63% / 96% after each CI are I-squared heterogeneity, not part of the interval.
citation.notes: Very high sensitivity → excellent rule-out; the algorithm also rules in. Verify before teaching use.

citation.reference: Cohen JF, et al. Rapid antigen detection test for group A streptococcus in children with pharyngitis. Cochrane Database Syst Rev. 2016;7:CD010502 (105 evaluations, 58,244 participants).
citation.sourceQuote: RADT had a summary sensitivity of 85.6%; 95% confidence interval (CI) 83.3 to 87.6 and a summary specificity of 95.4%; 95% CI 94.5 to 96.2.
citation.context: Children with pharyngitis, RADT vs throat culture; median GAS prevalence ~29.5%. Sensitivity is heterogeneous across studies.
citation.notes: High specificity (rule-in); imperfect sensitivity means a negative RADT may warrant culture in children. Verify before teaching use.

citation.reference: Sheppard AL, et al. Systematic review with meta-analysis: the accuracy of serological tests to support the diagnosis of coeliac disease. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2022;55(5):514-527.
citation.sourceQuote: Summary sensitivity and specificity of immunoglobulin A (IgA) anti-tissue transglutaminase were 90.7% (95% confidence interval: 87.3%, 93.2%) and 87.4% (84.4%, 90.0%) in adults (5 studies).
citation.context: Adults in secondary care vs duodenal biopsy; values differ in children (sens ~97.7%, spec ~70.2%). Requires adequate gluten intake and total-IgA sufficiency.
citation.notes: Adult values shown; pediatric characteristics differ. Verify before teaching use.

citation.reference: Zhang WC, Wu H, Chen WX. Meta-analysis: diagnostic accuracy of anti-CCP-2 and anti-CCP-3 antibody in rheumatoid arthritis. Clin Chem Lab Med. 2014;52(6):779-790 (17 studies).
citation.context: Anti-CCP-2 assay for known/suspected RA. High specificity, moderate sensitivity.
citation.notes: Classic rule-in test (high specificity); a negative result does not exclude RA. Verify before teaching use.

sequences.name: DVT: D-dimer → compression US
sequences.stopNote: In low pre-test probability, a NEGATIVE D-dimer typically excludes DVT; a positive D-dimer is followed by compression ultrasonography.
sequences.independenceNote: D-dimer (fibrin turnover) and ultrasonography (direct visualization) probe different biology, so conditional independence is a reasonable teaching approximation.

sequences.name: PE: D-dimer → CTPA
sequences.stopNote: In low/intermediate pre-test probability, a NEGATIVE high-sensitivity D-dimer typically ends the workup (PE excluded). CTPA is pursued when the D-dimer is positive or pre-test probability is high.
sequences.independenceNote: D-dimer (a biomarker of fibrin turnover) and CTPA (direct imaging) probe different biology, so treating them as conditionally independent is a reasonable teaching approximation.
